beats.reasoning: Morning routine at home; Isaac checks weather and realizes rain will persist until 2pm, so he plans accordingly. Low energy from yesterday (45/100) means a calm start.

beats.activity: Drive to Redmond job site, taking I-90 eastbound to avoid SR-520 closure.
beats.reasoning: SR-520 is closed eastbound 9am–noon; Isaac leaves early to beat the closure and avoid the backup, taking the longer but passable I-90 route.

beats.activity: Work on wind turbine maintenance and inspection on-site; steady rain keeps pace slow but safe.
beats.reasoning: Isaac works most of the morning in drizzle; rain from 7am–2pm means slower, more cautious work on the towers, so he wraps up by midday when conditions are worst.

beats.activity: Grab lunch at a nearby café in Redmond; stays dry indoors during peak rain.
beats.reasoning: Rain is heaviest midday; Isaac eats close to the job site to minimize exposure and mirrors yesterday's habit of a quick local lunch.

beats.reasoning: Rain ends around 2pm; Isaac is home early with low energy, so he does pottery indoors to decompress—mirrors yesterday's need to center himself with clay after physical work.

beats.reasoning: Isaac loves bookstores and uses them to decompress; 45 minutes of solo leisure time aligns with his low energy and habit of stopping by Elliott Bay.

diary:
Started the day watching rain hammer the windows at home—typical Seattle Tuesday. The SR-520 closure meant rerouting to Redmond via I-90, which added time but at least kept me moving. Work on the turbines was slower in the wet, but there's something grounding about doing maintenance in steady rain. Grabbed lunch at Sammamish Valley Café and just sat there warming my hands around coffee, watching the downpour.

By the time I got home, I needed to shake off the workday. Threw on dry clothes and got my hands in clay for a bit—that's where my head clears. Later I biked over to Elliott Bay Books despite the lingering drizzle. The staff knew me by name, which felt nice. Found a used copy of something I've been wanting. Rode back through Capitol Hill as the clouds broke open just a little, then met up at Judkins Park for some hoops with friends. The court was wet but the air had finally cleared. Felt lighter than this morning.
thought_process: The rain and bridge closure disrupted the usual flow, so I made practical choices early—checked the weather before leaving, took I-90 without hesitation. Work moved slower but I didn't rush it; safety matters more than speed on turbines. Lunch indoors was a deliberate choice to reset. By afternoon I was restless, so I leaned into what settles me: pottery, then books. The bike ride to Capitol Hill felt like reclaiming agency after being stuck in traffic and weather all day. Social time at the park was the right call—I needed people and movement after hours of focused work. The day felt fragmented by logistics, but I managed to stitch together moments that felt like mine.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
